sourceMovie.title: Ronin
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ronin works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Transporter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ronin clicked for you, The Transporter (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ronin?
faq.answer: Try The Transporter, Triple Frontier, From Paris with Love—each shares DNA with Ronin while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ronin?
faq.answer: Ronin (1998) is best described as Action, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ronin worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
